Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-07-05 21:05 UTC

OP

>Free tiers for Data Streams are winding down, with access terminating on September 1st, 2026.
>During this transition, rate limits may become stricter to avoid system congestion. We are encouraging the heavy users of the service to switch to a paid >subscription as soon as possible to avoid disruption. Visit the Self Service centre to get started.

You were told "pay in any currency you like and payment abstraction will automatically convert it to link".
But then they made it just a Stripe subscription that just pays USD straight into Chainlink Labs bank account, completely bypassing any "payment abstraction".

>BUT THEY'LL BUY LINK FOR THE RESERVE WITH THAT MONEY
No they won't - they're currently unlocking around 18 MILLION tokens per quarter. of that 18 million they sell around 15 million and put 3 million into their "reserve"
They don't actually "buy back" the tokens that go into the reserve


They promised 'staking' which turned out to be limited to 45 million tokens.
Then implied "we'll release staking v1 so everyone can stake" then completely backtracked on it.

They promised "everyone will pay for data feeds in link, or any payment will be converted to link"
Then it turned to be "actually we only accept Visa or Mastercard via Stripe"

They promised "we are going to buy back LINK for a strategic reserve"
Then it turned out that they were buying them from themselves at a zero cost

item.title: Index funds are dead — 18 replies — 72.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Index funds are dead

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-07-05 21:03 UTC

OP

Something like 70% of the American public has their money in the stock market.

Index fund investors (“Bogleheads bro!”) want you to believe that they’re all going to be rich because they put $500 a month into the market. This would essentially mean every single American would become rich just by DCAing into the US stock market.

Index investing is dead. The ONLY reason it worked in the past is because like 5% of Americans owned stocks. The ones that got in first benefited from everyone else piling in, exactly like Bitcoin.

Look at Bitcoin, all the Bitcoin retards made the same mistake of telling people it’ll just go up and up forever, now we’ve plateaued for the last 5 years and the Bitcoin trade is dead.

The only winners going forward will be good active investors. You’ve been warned.

Early replies (first 3)

62448308
It's all going to crash in the next 5 years, and economic pain will be unlike anything we've ever felt. So enjoy your gay stocks you fucking wealthsimple zoomer nigger.

62448320
>>62448306
It's so over for America saar! India superpower by 2030!

62448334
>>62448306
You're forgetting that since the 1970s and the birth of the Internet we've seen basically all major corporations become multi-national entities, access to the American markets overseas increasing, sovereign wealth funds worth trillions of dollars coming up with holding timelines that basically go to infinity since they're not constrained to the lifespan of a single person, etc.
The days of the USA markets being constrained to just America is fucking over. That's why the Buffett Indicator is such a worthless metric now. The USA has basically conquered the globe in terms of stocks, sure you'll get some thirdies teeth gnashing over this and crying about BRICS but fundamentally the Boglechads are still right and are still early.
>B-BUT THAT MEANS AMERICANS WITH STOCKS ARE GOING TO BE WEALTHY
Yeah, no shit. We're the best country on earth, of course we're wealthy.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62448339
Id still take index funds long term over any other investment vehicle even if the returns will be closer to 7% a year longterm. Thats about equal to the true rate of inflation

62448366
>>62448334
U niggers are printing our meme reserve currency.

62448372
>>62448334
>access to the American markets overseas increasing
This will gradually reverse as the disparity in access to capital between US and non-US firms widens.
It won't be a "huurr duur everyone dump on America" event, but policies will be tweaked and adjusted to make up for this difference. It already happens in some countries for pensions with providers being forced to invest in domestic companies. They will eventually apply the same "tax incentives" to retail investors too and push them towards domestic assets.

62448201
>>62447617

62448206
>>62444410
>Also how does it work if you leave the company? Here we get paid out our leave (not sick tho) in cash when we quit.
It's different state by state. In most states, companies don't have to pay out your vacation bank if you leave, but every time I had that I found the company was willing to pay it out.
On the unlimited side that means there's no bank to pay out, but once you get to that level it doesn't make much of a difference unless you leave willingly (at which point you probably got a signing bonus at the new job anyway) or if you get laid off you get a severance package, or if you were fired for misconduct or not doing your job they weren't going to pay it out anyway.

62448231
>>62447281
>nigger slave bragging about job security in the 1800s

62448269
i get 22 days + 12 company holidays + half day fridays in the summer. i don't know how many sick days i get, but i wfh so it's not a big deal.

62448433
In Denmark, PTO is handled through a national accrual tax systems rather than being fully granted upfront.

Standard full time employee:
>~2.08 vacation days per month worked (25 days/year)

I work for a tech company that offers extra 5 days:
>~0.42 vacation days per month worked (5 days/year)
>allowed to borrow up to 5 days

I can effectively use up to 35 days of PTO/year, but ofc I'm then at -5 so I'd have to work like 2 months just to get back to 0 PTO. If I were to quit I'd simply get less salary at the end.

Also, 1 PTO day for moving and unlimited sick days. I work an average of 30-35 hours a week.

62448461
UK private sector, 25 days of holiday, 8 bank holidays. We don't get allocated sick days, you just stay off work when you're sick. People really game the system, I'd guess the average person takes four or five sick days a year.

62448490
>>62443950
USA 2 weeks total sick/personal time. Please just put me out of my misery.

62448500
>>62443950
>Post country and how much PTO you get
4 weeks annual leave, will go up to 5 weeks in a few years. 3 weeks sick leave. I can carry over up to 6 weeks of annual leave year to year and can carry over infinite sick leave into the pool.

>>62443958
>If you’re white collar professional is the USA really a nightmare for employees like everyone says ? Or it’s over exaggerated?
Most people cry over nothing IMO but it depends on employer, some really are as bad as they as say, some are substantially better than anything you'll find in Europe too (tech, Healthcare mostly). For leave, I'd say 2-3 weeks of PTO total (both sick and annual leave) is common for 20-30 year olds as a professional for a corporation but it does go up generally with years of service. You will absolutely make significantly more than 95% of Europeans in a similar position with (potentially) a lower cost of living but will likely have to work more than 40 hours a week. I personally would rather make more money, take risks, and retire early so the US system is far superior to the European model but it depends on what value, Euros tend to value guarantees and low risk far more than I do.

item.title: /BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition — 128 replies — 27.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
/BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition

Open thread • Replies: 128 • Updated: 2026-07-05 16:23 UTC

OP

>rc buys 7,780,000 shares of BBBY, and $60-$80 strike calls worth 1,670,100 shares in March 2022

>rc sells shares and calls of BBBY in august 2022

>rc has a sitdown with a famous corporate raider, Carl Icahn in october 2022

>rc offers 400 million for BBBY in December 2022

>pitchbook (best M&A software suite in the world, costing 5 figures + per year) lists BBBY(now known as BBBYQ or DK-Butterfly-1) as sold via a leveraged buyout on January 13 2023

>a default on the ABL with JPM happened on the same day that pitchbook shows a leveraged buyout, the terms of that ABL specify that a default is triggered if a change of control happens, a change of control is defined as an acquisition of 40% or more of the voting power by any person or group.

>one month later, February 2023 RC tweets “someone tell the government to stop shooting down my balloons”, alluding to bad actors delaying the Bed Bath m&a

>the GameStop CEO then became a BBBYQ chapter 11 creditor and debtor

>Larry cheng (who helped RC build Chewy, and is a current boardmember at GameStop, and a close personal friend to RC) went onto theppshow podcast (which is dedicated to BBBY recovery theories) 8 months after BBBY filed for chapter 11, and 2 months after shares were cancelled

>OverStock - who is the most famous company to battle illegal naked shorts, who even released a crypto dividend 5 years ago - purchased the BBBY IP, and owns 55% of TZero - the only approved blockchain entity who can issue debt instruments and securities

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 202 replies — 10.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 202 • Updated: 2026-07-05 01:59 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

emperorBTC's telegram has a basic bitch tldr set of material to learn from. also has a youtube where he goes over stuff. that's where I started and I like that he focuses on the absolute basics, although he leans heavily towards confirmation w/ volume which others may disagree with. btc focused but btc/crypto is perhaps the most effective use of TA.
basically there's no need to get fancy and the chart is the final source of truth because what the price does > what people cope and/or seethe about
support/resistance levels and price action with risk management (defined r:r) = compound as you get gud
once you commit you'll realize "the catch" on people actually make money from trading is is simply how retarded the overwhelming majority of people are that they simply cannot manage a few simple tenets to click buttons and make money, you likely being one of them if you don't develop a process and risk too much on trades
gl anon

62428141
>>62428130
also perps have exploded so trading equities for crypto natives has been a pleasure to avoid tradfi gatekeeping bs
follow the right people on twatter with alerts set to on and it's like having multiple research firms giving alpha for free-- key word "right" accounts. hopefully you have the pattern recognition to figure out which are spewing paid channel larps and which are providing genuine insight
that's basically the whole game. if you can get into a group chat with other chads that's even better because a network is stronger than you alone, although this is def an arena where it ultimately comes down to you mastering yourself (you can't copy trade your way to wealth but you can learn what others do/how they enter/exit positions)
legend trade is a new perps platform that's gamifying this if you wanna start there instead of hyperliquid (front-end using hyperliquid)
Also bigtrout300 on twitter and join his tg as well he's pretty dope. There's a lot of great accounts posting good shit right now. Some really good posters fade in/out of cycles while the scammers/extractors are always abundant, but just use discretion
